sections.heading: Definition and Origin
sections.content: Neuroblastoma is a cancer that originates from the developing sympathetic nervous system and is the most common extracranial solid tumor in children [1,2]. It is a primitive neoplasm of neuroectodermal origin composed of neuroblasts, or immature nerve cells [5].

sections.heading: Anatomical Locations
sections.content: These tumors may occur anywhere in the sympathoadrenal neuroendocrine system, including the adrenal gland, connective/soft tissue, retroperitoneum, and mediastinum [5].

sections.heading: Neuroblastic Tumor Spectrum
sections.content: Neuroblastoma, ganglioneuroblastoma, and ganglioneuroma are tumors of the sympathetic nervous system that arise from the embryonic neural crest and are referred to collectively as neuroblastic tumors [1,2].

sections.heading: Clinical Heterogeneity
sections.content: The disease is considered a complex and heterogeneous entity due to differences in disease severity, symptoms, and clinical behavior; for example, some patients have tumors that spontaneously regress without any treatment, while others are diagnosed with aggressive metastatic disease requiring multimodal intervention [1,6].
